episode_summary: The team discuss how pretentious titles can make artworks seem more profound, why many modern Disney princesses appear ambidextrous, a Kurdish tailor who dressed both Saddam Hussein and Nelson Mandela, and how all-female desert grassland whiptail lizards trigger pregnancy by simulating mating.

facts.fact: My fact this week is that if you want to make your work of art more profound, give it a pretentious name. This is scientifically the case.
Okay, my fact this week is that most Disney princesses are ambidextrous.
My fact is, Saddam Hussein and Nelson Mandela had the same tailor.
My fact this week is that desert grassland whitetail lizards get pregnant by pretending to mate with each other.
